article_meta.title: Police turned him over to ICE

reasoning: The summary is largely neutral and avoids the article’s most emotive phrasing, with proper attribution for critiques of 287(g). Minor lapses include un-attributed use of “controversial” and the phrase “harsh realities,” and it underplays pro-287(g) perspectives present in the article.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (e.g., “opened fire,” “raised scrutiny”); avoids loaded verbs.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Mostly supported; “limited support” from ICE and “harsh realities” are somewhat interpretive.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Calls 287(g) “controversial” without attribution and notes “harsh realities”; otherwise attributes critiques.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Avoids emotive headline language; tone remains measured.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes critics and family hardship; under-emphasizes law enforcement arguments that agreements clear dangerous offenders.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Does not include the supportive perspective from sheriffs/police praising 287(g) as in the article.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Limited use of subjective terms like “controversial,” “harsh realities.”
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Generally neutral terminology; avoids politically charged labels.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Focus is on tragedy and policy criticism; supporting context is lighter than in the source.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Balances federal officials and family/advocates; no clear deference.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than source’s emotive narrative, with small residual framing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Multiple perspectives included, but pro-287(g) rationale is underrepresented compared to the source.
